markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Define how Monaco workers, parser workers, and visual-debug assets are served by Handshake without external CDNs or manually launched servers. This MT belongs to NativeDependencyAndPackaging. Make WP-009 native-by-default and fully packaged inside Handshake.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
No proof may require manually launched external services or tools except explicitly allowed model/GGUF/tensor/CUI-portable surfaces.
Bundled frontend/editor libraries must be lockfile-governed, license-reviewed, and callable through Handshake UI/runtime surfaces.
scope.expected_behavior: FrontendWorkerBundling leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: FrontendWorkerBundling is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: Implemented in wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 commit 77284b0a; proof commands green per delegate report (vitest/cargo/playwright; MT-032 aggregate check:dependency-policy 8/8 pass, app suite 313/313). Pending adversarial code review before READY_FOR_VALIDATION.

concerns: check-worker-bundling runs the REAL pnpm build + build:harness and scans the actual dist trees (not synthetic fixtures): app/dist (files scanned), app/dist-harness (5 monaco worker chunks, MonacoEnvironment marker detected). Zero external worker refs, zero unexempted CDN hits. This is genuine built-artifact evidence, NOT tautological.

The Excalidraw esm.sh exception is LOAD-BEARING in the shipped MAIN app: app/dist shows cdn_exceptions_applied:1 (exactly 1 esm.sh occurrence near ASSETS_FALLBACK_URL). The fallback string ships in the production bundle (dormant - fonts bundled as .woff2).

WEAKNESS: the 400-char proximity window is SYMMETRIC (+-400) and suppresses ANY esm.sh within it. An attacker who could influence the bundle near the ASSETS_FALLBACK_URL marker could hide a SECOND, live esm.sh reference within 400 chars and it would be silently exempted. The window is keyed to a string marker, not to the specific Excalidraw fallback occurrence.
evasion_probes: PROBE C (confirmed exploit): content with ASSETS_FALLBACK_URL + esm.sh/a, then 200 filler chars, then import('https://esm.sh/EVIL') -> partitionCdnHits returns violations:0 exempted:2. BOTH esm.sh refs suppressed because EVIL is within 400 chars of the marker.
PROBE C2 (control): same with 500 filler chars -> violations:1 exempted:1 (EVIL beyond 400 correctly flagged). Confirms the hole is exactly the proximity radius.
Real bundle has only 1 esm.sh occurrence today, so the exception is correct in the current build; the weakness is latent.
evidence_checked: pnpm run check:dependency-policy ran end-to-end with REAL builds (tsc+vite app/dist + build:harness app/dist-harness); exit 0; 8/8 checks pass. worker-bundling pass, built:true, app/dist cdn_exceptions_applied:1, dist-harness 5 worker chunks.
grep: exactly 1 'esm.sh' in app/dist (index-*.js) adjacent to ASSETS_FALLBACK_URL.
vitest 56/56 green across app/src/lib/dependency_policy/*, DependencyFailureBanner.test.tsx, worker_map.test.ts, extension_set.test.ts. frontend_worker_bundling.test.ts both-ways exception fixture.

evidence: Implementation commit 77284b0a on feat/WP-KERNEL-009; adversarial code review verdict HARDEN_RECOMMENDED -> remediated in commit 0bfcceb2, exploits independently re-run by reviewer and confirmed CLOSED; runtime evidence: check:dependency-policy 8/8 exit 0 (real builds), cargo --lib dependency_policy:: 31/31, vitest 67/67 post-hardening.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
